cultural.name: Demilitarized Zone
cultural.description: Return all structure on this planet to your reinforcements. Then, return all ground forces on this planet to the space area. ATTACH: Units cannot be committed to, produced on or placed on this planet. During the agenda phase, this planet's planet card can be traded as part of a transaction.

cultural.name: Dyson Sphere
cultural.description: ATTACH: This planet's resource value is increased by 2 and its influence value is increased by 1.

cultural.name: Freelancers
cultural.description: You may produce 1 unit in this system; you may spend influence as if it were resources to produce this unit.

cultural.name: Gamme Wormhole
cultural.description: Place a gamma wormhole token in this system. Then, purge this card.

cultural.name: Mercenary Outfit
cultural.description: You may place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on this planet.

cultural.name: Paradise World
cultural.description: ATTACH: This planet's influence value is increased by 2.

cultural.name: Tomb of Emphidia
cultural.description: ATTACH: This planet's influence value is increased by 1. If the player who has the 'Crown of Emphidia' Relic has control of this planet, they can use that relic to gain 1 Victory Point.

cultural.name: Cultural Relic Fragment
cultural.description: ACTION: Purge 3 of your cultural relic fragments to gain 1 Relic.

hazardous.name: Core Mine
hazardous.description: If you have at least 1 mech on this planet, or if you remove 1 infantry form this planet, gain 1 trade good.

hazardous.name: Expedition
hazardous.description: If oyu have at least 1 mech on this planet, or if you remove 1 infantry from this , ready this planet.

hazardous.name: Lazax Survivors
hazardous.description: ATTACH: This planet's resource value is increased by 1 and its influence value is increased by 2.

hazardous.name: Mining World
hazardous.description: ATTACH: This planet's resource value is increased by 2.

hazardous.name: Rich World
hazardous.description: ATTACH: This planet's resource value is increased by 1.

hazardous.name: Volatile Fuel Source
hazardous.description: If you have at least 1 mech on this planet, or if you remove 1 infantry form this planet, gain 1 command token.

hazardous.name: Warfare Research Facility
hazardous.description: ATTACH: This planet has a red technology specialty; if this planet already has a technology specialty, this planet's resource and influence values are each increased by 1 instead.

hazardous.name: Hazardous Relic Fragment
hazardous.description: ACTION: Purge 3 of your hazardous relic fragments to gain 1 Relic.

industrial.name: Abandoned Warehouses
industrial.description: You may gain 2 commodities, or you may convert up to 2 of your commodities to trade goods.

industrial.name: Biotic Research Facility
industrial.description: ATTACH: This planet has a green technology specialty; if this planet already has a technology specialty, this planet's resource and influence values are each increased by 1 instead.

industrial.name: Cybernetic Research Facility
industrial.description: ATTACH: This planet has a yellow technology specialty; if this planet already has a technology specialty, this planet's resource and influence values are each increased by 1 instead.

industrial.name: Funcitoning Base
industrial.description: You may gian 1 commodity, or you may spend 1 trade good or 1 commodity to draw 1 action card.

industrial.name: Local Fabricators
industrial.description: You may gian 1 commodity, or you may spend 1 trade good or 1 commodity to place 1 mech from your reinforcements on this planet.

industrial.name: Propulsion Research Facility
industrial.description: ATTACH: This planet has a blue technology specialty; if this planet already has a technology specialty, this planet's resource and influence values are each increased by 1 instead.

industrial.name: Industrial Relic Fragment
industrial.description: ACTION: Purge 3 of your industrial relic fragments to gain 1 Relic.

frontier.name: Enigmatic Device
frontier.description: Place this card face up in your play area. ACTIONL You may spend 6 resources and purge this card to research 1 technology.

frontier.name: Gamma Relay
frontier.description: Place a gamma wormhole token in this system. Then, purge this card.

frontier.name: Ion Storm
frontier.description: Place the ion storm token in this system with either side face up. Then, place this card in the common play area. At the end of a 'Move Ships' or 'Retreat' sub-step of a tactical action during which 1 or more of your ships use the ion storm wormhole, flip the ion storm token to its opposing side.

frontier.name: Merchant Station
frontier.description: You may replenish your commodities, or you may convert your commodities to trade goods.

frontier.name: Mirage
frontier.description: Place the Mirage planet token in this system. Gain the Mirage planet card and ready it. Then, purge this card.

frontier.name: Unknown Relic Fragment
frontier.description: This card counts as a relic fragment of any type.

frontier.name: Entropic Field
frontier.description: Gain 1 command token and 2 trade goods.

frontier.name: Major Entropic Field
frontier.description: Gain 1 command token and 3 trade goods.

frontier.name: Minor Entropic Field
frontier.description: Gain 1 command token and 1 trade good.

pok.name: Dominus Orb
pok.description: Before you move units during a tactical action, you may purge this card to move and transport units that are in systems that contain 1 of your command tokens.

pok.name: Maw of Worlds
pok.description: At the start of the agenda phase, you may purge this card and exhaust all of your planets to gain any 1 technology.

pok.name: Scepter of Emelpar
pok.description: When you would spend a token form your strategy pool, you may exhaust this card to spend a token from your reinforcements instead.

pok.name: Shard of the Throne
pok.description: When you gain this card, gain 1 victory point. When you lose this card, lose 1 victory point. When a player gains control of a legendary planet you control, or a planet you control in your home system, that player gains this card.

pok.name: Stellar Converter
pok.description: ACTION: Choose 1 non-home, non-legendary planet other than Mecatol Rex in a system that is adjacent to 1 or more of your units that have BOMBARDMENT; destroy all units on that planet and purge its attachments and its planet card. Then, place the destroyed planet token on that planet and purge this card.

pok.name: The Codex
pok.description: ACTION: Purge this card to take up to 3 action cards of your choice from the action card discard pile.

pok.name: The Crown of Emphidia
pok.description: After you perform a tactical action, you may exhaust this card to explore 1 planet you control. At the end of the status phase, if you control the 'Tomb of Emphidia', you may purge this card to gian 1 victory point.

pok.name: The Crown of Thalnos
pok.description: During each combat round, this card's owner may reroll any number of their dice, applying +1 to the results; any units that reroll dice but do not produce at least 1 hit are destroyed.

pok.name: The Obsidian
pok.description: When you gain this card, draw 1 secret objective. You can have 1 additional scored or unscored secret objective.

pok.name: The Prophet's Tears
pok.description: When you research a technology, you may exhaust this card to ignore 1 prerequisite or draw 1 action card.

codices.name: Dynamis Core
codices.description: While this card is in your play area, your commodity value is increased by 2. ACTION: Purge this card to gain trade goods equal to your printed commodity value +2.

codices.name: JR-XS455-0
codices.description: ACTION: Exhaust this agent and choose a player; that player may spend 3 resources to place a structure on a plent they control. If they do not, they gian 1 trade good.

codices.name: Nano-Forge
codices.description: ACTION: Attach this card to a non-legendary, non-home planet you control; its resource and influence values are increased by 2 and it is a legendary planet.

codices.name: Circlet of the Void
codices.description: Your units do not roll for gravity rifts, and you ignore the movement effects of other anomalies. ACTION: Exhaust this card to explore a frontier token in a system that does not contain any other players' ships.

codices.name: Book of Latvinia
codices.description: When you gain this card, research up to 2 technologies that have no prerequsites. ACTION: Purge this card; if you control planets that have all 4 types of technology specialties, gain 1 victory point. Otherwise, gain the speaker token.

codices.name: Neuraloop
codices.description: When a public objective is revealed, you may purge one of your relics to discard that objective and replace it with a random objective from any objective deck; that objective is a public objective, even if it is a secret objective.

te.name: Metali Void Armaments
te.description: During the 'Anti-Fighter Barrage' step of space combat, you may resolve ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE 6(x3) against your opponent's units.

te.name: The Quantumcore
te.description: When you gain this card, gain your breakthrough. You have SYNERGY for all technology types.

te.name: The Silver Flame
te.description: The Silver Flame can be exchanged as part of a transaction. ACTION: Roll 1 die and purge this card; if the result is a 10, gain 1 victory point. Otherwise, purge your home system and all units in it; you cannot score public objectives. Put the Fracture into play if it is not already.

te.name: Lightrail Ordinance
te.description: Your space docks gain SPACE CANNON 5(x2). You may use your space dock's SPACE CANNON against ships that are adjacent to their system.

te.name: Metali Void Shielding
te.description: Each time hits are produced against 1 of your non-fighter ships, 1 of ythose ships may use SUSTAIN DAMAGE as if it had that ability.

te.name: The Triad
te.description: This card can be readied and spent as if it were a planet card. Its resource and influence values are equal to 3 plus the number of different types of relic fragments you own.

te.name: Heart of Ixth
te.description: After any die is rolled, you may exhaust this card to add or subtract 1 from its results.

base.name: Ancient Burial Sites
base.note: At the start of the agenda phase:
base.description: Choose 1 player. Exhaust each cultural planet owned by that player.

base.name: Assassinate Representative
base.description: Choose 1 player. That player cnanot vote on this agenda.

base.name: Bribery
base.note: After the speaker votes on an agenda:
base.description: Spend any number of trade goods. For each trade good spent, cast 1 additional vote for the outcome on which you voted.

base.name: Bunker
base.note: At the start of an invasion:
base.description: During this invasion, apply -4 to the result of each Bombardment roll against planets you control.

base.name: Confusing Legal Text
base.note: When you are elected as the outcome of an agenda:
base.description: Choose 1 player. That player is the elected player instead.

base.name: Construction Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud the outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, place 1 space dock from your reinforcements on a planet you control.

base.name: Courageous to the End
base.note: After 1 of your ships is destroyed during a space combat:
base.description: Roll 2 dice. For each result equal to or greater than that ship's combat value, your opponent must choose and destroy 1 of their ships.

base.name: Cripple Defenses
base.description: Destroy each PDS on that planet.

base.name: Diplomacy Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, choose 1 system that contains a planet you control. Each other player places a command token from their reinforcements in that system.

base.name: Direct Hit
base.note: After another player's ship uses Sustain Damage to cancel a hit produced by your units or abilities:
base.description: Destroy that ship.

base.name: Disable
base.note: At the start of an invasion in a system that contains 1 or more of your opponents' PDS units:
base.description: Your opponents' PDS units lose Planetary Shield and Space Cannon during this invasion.

base.name: Distinguished Councilor
base.note: After you cast votes on an outcome of an agenda:
base.description: Cast 5 additional votes for that outcome.

base.name: Economic Initiative
base.description: Ready each cultural planet you control.

base.name: Emergency Repairs
base.note: At the start of a combat round:
base.description: Repair all of your units that have Sustain Damage in the active system.

base.name: Experimental Battlestation
base.note: After another player moves ships into a system during a tactical action:
base.description: Choose 1 of your space docks that is either in or adjacent to that system. That space dock uses Space Cannon 5(x3) against ships in the active system.

base.name: Fighter Prototype
base.note: At the start of the first round of space combat:
base.description: Apply +2 to the result of each of your fighters' combat rolls during this combat round.

base.name: Fire Team
base.note: After your ground forces make combat rolls during a round of ground combat:
base.description: Reroll any number of your dice.

base.name: Flank Speed
base.description: Apply +1 to the move value of each of your ships during this tactical action.

base.name: Focused Research
base.description: Spend 4 trade goods to research 1 technology.

base.name: Frontline Deployment
base.description: Place 3 infantry from your reinforcements on 1 planet you control.

base.name: Ghost Ship
base.description: Place 1 destroyer from your reinforcements in a non-home system that contains a wormhole and does not contain other players' ships.

base.name: Imperial Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, gain 1 victory point.

base.name: In The Silence Of Space
base.description: Choose 1 system. During this tactical action, your ships in the chosen system can move through systems that contain other players' ships.

base.name: Industrial Initiative
base.description: Gain 1 trade good for each industrial planet you control.

base.name: Infiltrate
base.note: When you gain control of a planet:
base.description: Replace each PDS and space dock that is on that planet with a matching unit from your reinforcements.

base.name: Insubordination
base.description: Remove 1 token from another player's tactic pool and return it to their reinforcements.

base.name: Intercept
base.note: After your opponent declares a retreat during a space combat:
base.description: Your opponent cannot retreat during this round of space combat.

base.name: Leadership Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, gain 3 command tokens.

base.name: Lost Star Chart
base.description: During this tactical action, systems that contain alpha and beta wormholes are adjacent to each other.

base.name: Lucky Shot
base.description: Destroy 1 dreadnought, cruiser, or destroyer in a system that contains a planet you control.

base.name: Maneuvering Jets
base.note: Before you assign hits produced by another player's Space Cannon roll:
base.description: Cancel 1 hit.

base.name: Mining Initiative
base.description: Gain trade goods equal to the resource value of 1 planet you control.

base.name: Morale Boost
base.note: At the start of a combat round:
base.description: Apply +1 to the result of each of your unit's combat rolls during this combat round.

base.name: Parley
base.note: After another player commits units to land on a planet you control:
base.description: Return the committed units to the space area.

base.name: Plague
base.note: ACTION: Choose 1 planet that is controlled by another player.
base.description: Roll 1 die for each infantry on that planet. For each result of 6 or greater, destroy 1 of those units.

base.name: Political Stability
base.note: When you would return your strategy card(s) during the status phase:
base.description: Do not return your strategy card(s). You do not choose strategy cards during the next strategy phase.

base.name: Politics Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, draw 3 action cards and gain the speaker token.

base.name: Public Disgrace
base.note: When another player chooses a strategy card during the strategy phase:
base.description: That player must choose a different strategy card instead, if able.

base.name: Reactor Meltdown
base.description: Destroy 1 space dock in a non-home system.

base.name: Reparations
base.note: After another player gains control of a planet you control:
base.description: Exhaust 1 planet that player controls and ready 1 planet you control.

base.name: Repeal Law
base.description: Discard 1 law from play.

base.name: Rise of a Messiah
base.description: Place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on each planet you control.

base.name: Sabotage
base.note: When another player plays an action card other than 'Sabotage':
base.description: Cancel that action card.

base.name: Salvage
base.note: After you win a space combat:
base.description: Your opponent gives you all of their commodities.

base.name: Shields Holding
base.note: Before you assign hits to your ships during a space combat:
base.description: Cancel up to 2 hits.

base.name: Signal Jamming
base.description: Choose 1 non-home system that contains or is adjacent to 1 of your ships. Place a command token form another player's reinforcements in that system.

base.name: Skilled Retreat
base.note: At the start of a combat round:
base.description: Move all of your ships from the active system into an adjacent system that does not contain another player's ships; the space combat ends in a draw. Then, place a command token from your reinforcements in that system.

base.name: Spy
base.description: Choose 1 player. That player gives you 1 random action card form their hand.

base.name: Summit
base.note: At the start of the strategy phase:
base.description: Gain 2 command tokens.

base.name: Tactical Bombardment
base.description: Choose 1 system that contains 1 or more of your units that have Bombardment. Exhaust each planet controlled by other players in that system.

base.name: Technology Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, research 1 technology.

base.name: Trade Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, gain 5 trade goods.

base.name: Unexpected Action
base.description: Remove 1 of your command tokens from the game board and return it to your reinforcements.

base.name: Unstable Planet
base.description: Choose 1 hazardous planet. Exhaust that planet and destroy up to 3 infantry on it.

base.name: Upgrade
base.note: After you activate a system that contains 1 or more of your ships:
base.description: Replace one of your cruisers in that system with one of your dreadnoughts from your reinforcements.

base.name: Uprising
base.description: Exhaust 1 non-home planet controlled by another player. Then gain trade goods equal to its resource value.

base.name: Veto
base.description: Discard that agenda and reveal 1 agenda from the top of the deck. Players vote on this agenda instead.

base.name: War Effort
base.description: Place 1 cruiser from your reinforcements in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships.

base.name: Warfare Rider
base.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediction is correct, place 1 dreadnought from your reinforcements in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships.

pok.name: Archaeological Expedition
pok.description: Reveal the top 3 cards of an exploration deck that matches a planet you control; gain any relic fragments that you reveal and discard the rest.

pok.name: Confounding Legal Text
pok.note: When another player is elected as the outcome of an agenda:
pok.description: You are the elected player instead.

pok.name: Coup d'Etat
pok.note: When another player would perform a strategic action:
pok.description: End that player's turn; the strategic action is not resolved and the strategy card is not exhausted.

pok.name: Deadly Plot
pok.note: During the agenda phase, when an outcome would be revealed:
pok.description: If you voted for or predicted another outcome, discard the agenda instead: the agenda is resolved with no effect and it is not replaced. Then, exhaust all of your planets.

pok.name: Decoy Operation
pok.note: After another player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your structures:
pok.description: Remove up to 2 of your ground forces from the game board and place them on a planet you control in the actve system.

pok.name: Diplomatic Pressure
pok.description: Choose another player; that player must give you 1 promissory note form their hand.

pok.name: Divert Funding
pok.description: Return a non-unit upgrade, non-faction technology you own to your technology deck. Then, research another technology.

pok.name: Exploration Probe
pok.description: Explore a frontier token that is in or adjacent to a system that contains 1 or more of your ships.

pok.name: Manipulate Investments
pok.note: At the start of the strategy phase:
pok.description: Place a total of 5 trade goods from the supply on strategy cards of your choice; you must place these tokens on at least 3 different cards.

pok.name: Nav Suite
pok.description: During the 'Movement' step of this tactical action, ignore the effects of anomalies.

pok.name: Refit Troops
pok.description: Choose 1 or 2 of your infantry on the game board; replace each of those infantry wih mechs.

pok.name: Reveal Prototype
pok.note: At the start of a combat:
pok.description: Spend 4 resources to research a unit upgrade technology of the same type as 1 of your units that is participating in this combat.

pok.name: Reverse Engineer
pok.note: When another player discards an action card that has a component action:
pok.description: Take that action card from the discard pile.

pok.name: Rout
pok.note: At the start of the 'Announce Retreats' step of space combat, if you are the defender:
pok.description: Your opponent must announce a retreat, if able.

pok.name: Scuttle
pok.description: Choose 1 or 2 of your non-fighter ships on the game board and return them to your reinforcements; gain trade goods equal to the combined cost of those ships.

pok.name: Seize Artifact
pok.description: Choose 1 of your neighbours that has 1 or more relic fragments. That player must give you 1 relic fragment of your choice.

pok.name: Waylay
pok.note: Before you roll dice for ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE:
pok.description: Hits from this roll are produced against all ships (not just fighter).

codices.name: Blitz
codices.note: At the start of an invasion:
codices.description: Each of your non-fighter ships in the active system that do not have BOMBARDMENT gain BOMBARDMENT 6 until the end of the invasion.

codices.name: Counterstroke
codices.note: After a player activates a system that contains 1 of your command tokens:
codices.description: Return that command token to your tactic pool.

codices.name: Fighter Conscription
codices.description: Place 1 fighter from your reinforcements in each system that contains 1 or more of your space docks or units that have capacity; they cannot be placed in systems that cotain another player's ships.

codices.name: Forward Supply Base
codices.note: After another player activates a system that contains your units:
codices.description: Gain 3 trade goods. Then, choose another player to gain 1 trade good.

codices.name: Ghost Squad
codices.note: After a player commits units to land on a planet you control:
codices.description: Move any number of ground forces from any planet you control in the active system to any other planet you control in the active system.

codices.name: Hack Election
codices.description: During this agenda, voting begins with the speaker and continues counterclockwise.

codices.name: Harness Energy
codices.description: Replenish your commodities.

codices.name: Impersonation
codices.description: Spend 3 influence to draw 1 secret objective.

codices.name: Insider Information
codices.description: Look at the top card of the agenda deck.

codices.name: Master Plan
codices.description: You may perform an additional action this turn.

codices.name: Plagiarize
codices.description: Spend 5 influence and choose a non-faction technology owned by 1 of your neighbours; gain that technology.

codices.name: Rally
codices.note: After you ativate a system that contains another player's ships:
codices.description: Place 2 command tokens from your reinforcements in your fleet pool.

codices.name: Reflective Shielding
codices.note: Whenone of your ships uses SUSTAIN DAMAGE during combat:
codices.description: Produce 2 hits against your opponent's ships in the active system.

codices.name: Sanction:
codices.description: You cannot vote on this agenda. Predict aloud an outcome of this agenda. If your prediciton is correct, each player that voted for that outcome returns 1 command token from their fleet supply to their reinforcements.

codices.name: Scramble Frequency
codices.note: After another player makes a BOMBARDMENT, SPACE CANNON, or ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE roll:
codices.description: That player rerolls all of their dice.

codices.name: Solar Flare
codices.description: During movement, other players cannot use SPACE CANNON against your ships.

codices.name: War Machine
codices.note: When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION:
codices.description: Apply +4 to the total PRODUCTION value of your units and reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1.

te.name: Black Market Dealings
te.note: When you are negotiating a transaction:
te.description: You and the other player can include relics, action cards, and unscored secret objectives as part of the transaction. This card cannot be cancelled.

te.name: Brilliance
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Ready 1 of your planets that has a technology specialty or choose 1 player to gain their breakthrough.

te.name: Crash Landing
te.note: When your last ship in the active system is destroyed:
te.description: Place 1 of your ground forces from the space area of the active system onto a planet in that system other than Mecatol Rex; if the planet contains other players' units, place your ground force into coexistence.

te.name: Crisis
te.note: At the end of a player's turn, if there are at least 2 players who have not passed:
te.description: Skip the next player's turn.

te.name: Exchange Program
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Choose another player. you and that player may agree to place 1 infantry from your reinforcements into coexistence on a planet the other player controls that conains their ground forces; if no agreement is reached, you each discard 1 token from your fleet pool.

te.name: Extreme Duress
te.note: At the start of another player's turn, if they have a readied strategy card:
te.description: If that player's next action is not a strategic action, they discard all of their action cards, give you all of their trade goods, and show you all of their secret objectives.

te.name: Lie In Wait
te.note: After 2 of your neighbours resolve a transaction:
te.description: Look at each of those player's hands of action cards, then choose and take 1 action card from each.

te.name: Mercenary Contract
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Spend 2 trade goods to place 2 neutral infantry on any non-home planet that contains no units; if that planet was controlled by another player, they return its planet card to the planet card deck.

te.name: Pirate Contract
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Place 1 neutral destroyer in a non-home system that contains no non-neutral ships.

te.name: Pirate Fleet
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Spend 3 resources to place 1 neutral carrier, 1 neutral cruiser, 1 neutral destroyer, and 2 neutral fighters in a non-home system that contains no non-neutral ships.

te.name: Puppets on a String
te.note: At the end of any player's turn, if you have passed:
te.description: Perform 1 action.

te.name: Rescue
te.note: After another player moves ships into a system that contains your ships:
te.description: You may move 1 of your ships into the active system from any system that does not contain one of your command tokens.

te.name: Strategize
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Perform the secondary ability of any readied or unchosen strategy card.

te.name: Overrule
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Perform the primary ability of any readied or unchosen strategy card.

te.name: Master Plan Ω
te.note: After you perform an action:
te.description: Perform an additional action.

te.name: Fighter Conscription Ω
te.note: ACTION:
te.description: Place 1 fighter from your reinforcements in each system that contains 1 or more of your space docks or ships that have a capacity value; they cannot be placed in system that contain other player's ships.

te.name: Blitz Ω
te.note: At the start of an invasion:
te.description: Each of your non-fighter ships in the active system that does not have BOMBARDMENT gains BOMBARDMENT 6 until the end of the invasion.

te.name: Hack Election Ω
te.note: After an agenda is revealed:
te.description: During this agenda, you vote last.

te.name: Solar Flare Ω
te.note: After you activate a system:
te.description: During the 'Movement' step of this tactical action, other players cannot use SPACE CANNON against your ships.

te.name: Counterstroke Ω
te.note: After another player activates a system that contains 1 of your command tokens:
te.description: Return that command token to your tactic pool.

te.name: Ghost Squad Ω
te.note: After another player commits units to land on a planet you control:
te.description: Move any number of ground forces from any planet you control in the active system to any other planet you control in the active system.

te.name: War Machine Ω
te.note: When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION:
te.description: Apply +4 to the total PRODUCTION value of those units and reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1.

base.name: Anti-Intellectual Revolution
base.description:
FOR: After a player researches a technology, they must destroy 1 of their non-fighter ships.

AGAINST: At the start of the next strategy phase, each player chooses and exhausts 1 planet for each technology they own.

base.name: Classified Document Leaks
base.description:
When this agenda is revealed, if there are no scored secret, discard this card and reveal another agenda from the top of the deck.

The elected secret objective becomes a public objective; place it near the other public objectivesin the common play area.

base.name: Committee Formation
base.description: The elected player gains this card. Before players vote on an agenda that requires a player to be elected, the owner of this card may discard this card to choose a player to be elected. Players do not vote on this agenda.

base.name: Conventions of War
base.description:
FOR: Player cannot use BOMBARDMENT against units that are on cultural planets.

AGAINST: Each player that voted 'Against' discard all of their action cards.

base.name: Core Mining
base.description: Attach this card to the elected planet's card. Then, destroy 1 infantry on the planet. The resource value of this planet is increased by 2.

base.name: Demilitarized Zone
base.description: Attach this card to the elected planet's card. Then, destroy all units on that planet. Player's units cannot land, be produced, or be placed on this planet.

base.name: Enforced Travel Ban
base.description:
FOR: Alpha and Beta wormholes have no effect during movement.

AGAINST: Desstroy each PDS in or adjacent to a system tha contains a wormhole.

base.name: Executive Sanctions
base.description:
FOR: Each player can have a maximum of 3 action cards in their hand.

AGAINST: Each player discards 1  random action card from their hand.

base.name: Fleet Regulations
base.description:
FOR: Each player cannot have more than 4 command tokens in their fleet pool.

AGAINST: Each player places 1 command token from their reinforcements in their fleet pool.

base.name: Holy Planet of Ixth
base.description: Attach this card to the elected planet's card. The planet's owner gains 1 victory point. Units on this planet cannot use PRODUCTION. When a player gains control of this planet, they gain 1 victory point. When a player loses control of this planet, they lose 1 victory point.

base.name: Homeland Defense Act
base.description:
FOR: Each player can have any number of PDS units on planets they control.

AGAINST: Each player destroys 1 of their PDS units.

base.name: Imperial Arbiter
base.description: The elected player gains this card. At the end of the strategy phase, the owner of this card may discard this card to swap 1 of their strategy cards with 1 of another player's strategy cards.
